operating_state: Caterpillar achieved $67.060 billion in sales and revenues for 2023, representing 13% growth driven by favorable pricing and higher volumes across all segments. Operating profit margin expanded dramatically to 19.3% from 13.3% in 2022, driven by price realization exceeding manufacturing cost increases and operational efficiencies. Order backlog decreased to $27.5 billion from $30.4 billion as the company worked through elevated demand while managing supply chain constraints.
cost_structure: Cost of goods sold increased but improved as a percentage of sales due to higher volumes and disciplined pricing. Operating profit surged 64% to $12.966 billion with margin expansion primarily driven by favorable price realization exceeding manufacturing cost increases including higher material costs. Total operating expenses increased approximately $300 million reflecting strategic investments and elevated short-term incentive compensation rising to $1.7 billion.
material_events: The company completed divestiture of its Longwall business in Q1 2023, resulting in $586 million in restructuring costs and a $572 million loss primarily from foreign currency translation release. Share repurchases totaled $4.975 billion under the $15 billion authorization with $7.8 billion remaining available, while dividends maintained at $1.30 per share quarterly. Key leadership changes included Joseph Creed appointed Chief Operating Officer and Jason Kaiser named Group President of Energy & Transportation.
macro_exposures: Foreign exchange sensitivity indicates a hypothetical 10% U.S. dollar weakening would adversely affect expected 2024 cash flow by approximately $200 million, with primary exposures to Chinese yuan, Australian dollar, Euro, British pound and Mexican peso. The company faces continued supply chain disruptions, inflationary cost pressures, and labor constraints particularly affecting transportation and components. Economic uncertainty in Europe and China excavator market weakness present regional headwinds, while commodity price volatility affects mining and energy customer capital discipline.
forward_outlook: Management expects 2024 sales and revenues broadly similar to 2023 levels supported by healthy underlying demand and slight favorable price realization, with services growth anticipated across all segments. Construction Industries equipment sales expected flat while Resource Industries faces lower sales volume primarily in off-highway and articulated trucks. Capital expenditures projected at $2.0-2.5 billion with restructuring costs and effective tax rate guidance maintained, while focusing on operational excellence and strategic capital allocation.
uncertainty_profile: Unrecognized tax benefits totaled $1.223 billion with ongoing examinations creating contingent liabilities across multiple jurisdictions. Product warranty liability and post-sale discount reserves remain subject to variance if actual claims differ from historical estimates. Cat Financial maintains credit facility covenant compliance with leverage ratios well within required thresholds, while finance receivables portfolio reached $22.4 billion with past due amounts increasing year-over-year.
